# Build Young

> A live, hands-on program for teenagers (ages 15–18) that teaches them to build economic value. Tagline: "Raising builders, not consumers." Students first build something they believe people would pay for — that becomes their income — then learn to manage what they earn. In an AI world, the edge isn't a degree; it's what you can build.

## About
Build Young is an online program founded by Sunil Garg that teaches teens to build economic value and think like founders. First they build a product they believe people would pay for (a small product, app, or service made with AI) — and that becomes their income; then they learn to manage what they earn: business taxes, saving, investing so it compounds, big purchases, and budgeting. It runs as a 12-week course meeting twice a week (~3 hours/week), delivered live over Zoom, inside an interactive simulation where income is earned by the student's build. The program is financial education, not licensed financial advice; all money in the simulation is simulated.

## Who it's for
Teens ages 15–18 (the "Builders" program). Each season you choose a schedule — Mondays & Wednesdays, or Tuesdays & Thursdays:
- Builders program (ages 15–18) — $999

## What students learn
Act 1 — 0 to 1: build the first version (Weeks 1–6):
- Find a real problem people will pay to solve, and write a clear spec
- Build the product with AI (Claude Code) and keep building it solid
- Prioritize the feedback that rolls in — bugs vs. feature requests
Act 2 — 1 to 100: scale it into a business (Weeks 7–9):
- Understand your funnel: how people find you, try you, and stick
- Scale and grow what's working — repeatable ways to reach more people
Act 3 — Manage what you've earned (Weeks 10–11) + a capstone (Week 12):
- Saving, investing, diversification, and compounding through real market moves
- Big purchases: buying and financing a home and a car; good vs. bad debt
- A capstone tallying what you built and what it's worth

## Enrolling
Families can book a free 15-minute call with the founder before enrolling. A prorated refund is available through the end of the first 3 weeks.
